reflection_strategy.why_atomic_or_separable: The core problem is the r_progress formula. A single edit to its expression can remove the per‑step existence penalty while preserving stabilising incentives. Terminal rewards may later need rebalancing, but the immediate fix can stand alone.
editor_constraints: Output only one edit to r_progress; do NOT add new components yet.
Do NOT remove or disable event rules (successful_landing, crash).
Change the formula so that it does NOT penalise altitude (y) when not on the ground, or use a difference‑based potential to make dense reward non‑decreasing until terminal event.
Do not introduce positive rewards that can be accumulated indefinitely.
Maintain the existing weighting unless the formula change naturally changes dominance.
must_preserve: The stability component (r_stability) and control cost (r_control_cost) as they are currently.
The terminal event rules and their one‑time nature.
The overall negativity of the dense reward to avoid infinite reward exploits.
must_avoid: Any form of per‑step penalty that grows with episode length when the agent is making progress towards a safe landing.
Edits that would further shorten the episode by making crashing even more attractive.
Increasing the weight of terminal rewards without first fixing the dense shaping, as the agent may still pursue early termination to avoid the negative shaping.
reflection_strategy.expected_effect: The dominance of r_progress should drop, and the agent will no longer be incentivised to end episodes early by crashing. It will then begin to explore learning to land softly, driven primarily by the terminal success reward and the velocity/angular penalties. Task proxies such as safe_contact_rate and stable_landing_rate should rise, while episode length should increase as the agent attempts more careful landings.
reflection_strategy.risk_analysis: If the y‑term is simply removed without adding direction to descend, the agent may hover in place or fly too high, never attempting to land. The editor must ensure the edit still provides a downward incentive without punishing time spent on the descent. Removing all position penalties might leave no guidance toward the pad. The edit must therefore carefully reshape r_progress to encourage approach to (0,0) with low velocities, but only when close to the ground, or use a potential that resets at each episode start.
